events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: respirator wrong cartridge color (ID). This is a PPE selection issue. Wrong cartridge color means the respirator cartridge is not appropriate for the hazard (color coding indicates type of protection). This is non-compliant. Work stage is rough-complete. Decision: fail. Risk: high (respiratory protection failure). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 80. Value 0. Confidence high. Findings: wrong cartridge color for respirator, not matching hazard. Actions: replace with correct cartridge. References: OSHA 1910 Subpart I (respiratory protection), NFPA 70E maybe less relevant but included.

  "rationale": "Respirator cartridge color denotes the contaminant class the cartridge protects against. Using the wrong cartridge color means the wearer may have no effective protection for the actual hazard, which is a direct violation of OSHA PPE/respiratory protection requirements.",
